methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Epoch Biodesign is a Series A-stage biotech company that has raised $40M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 3 public peers in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, and Recursion Pharmaceuticals.

Only one valuation method proved applicable given data constraints. The Stage-Progression approach uses the company's $40M total raised as a baseline proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage step-ups, implying a valuation of $80M. The Peer-Median method was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or similarly-tiered comparable companies—while 8 biotech peers exist in the reference set, none met the narrow filtering criteria. Public-Comp Anchoring could not be applied because the target's revenue is not on file; the 3 public biotech comparables trade at a 6.9x median P/S, but this multiple cannot be deployed without disclosed revenue figures.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $68M to $92M, centered at $80M. This range reflects Stage-Progression as the sole triangulation point, with the bounds representing reasonable upside and downside variance. Key uncertainties include the absence of early-stage peer multiples specific to Epoch's therapeutic area and stage, the lack of revenue data limiting public-comp applicability, and limited visibility into the company's clinical or product-development milestones relative to private peers.
